content: {"answer": "The search results do not provide a direct head-to-head incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (ICER) of intermittent fasting versus metformin and GLP-1 receptor agonists for type 2 diabetes prevention over a 10-year horizon. No source reports a three-way comparison with cost-per-QALY estimates for this specific combination. However, adjacent evidence offers partial insight. Source 2 (Habka 2025) evaluated a fasting-mimicking diet (FMD), the closest analog to intermittent fasting in the results, and reported the FMD program was associated with 11.4% fewer diabetes complications and 67.2% less overall diabetes medication use versus usual care, supporting favorable cost-utility. Source 1 and Source 4 (van den Burg 2025) similarly performed trial-based and lifetime cost-effectiveness modeling of a one-year monthly 5-day FMD program in patients with type 2 diabetes, but the reported excerpts do not disclose the ICER figure. For lifestyle-based benchmarks, Source 9 (Duijzer 2019) cites a systematic review median ICER of $13,761/QALY for diet and physical activity programs from a health care perspective, and Source 10 reports nonpharmacologic diet/exercise interventions at $25,400 to $57,400 per QALY, indicating good value. Source 8 (Ma 2020) found lifestyle intervention averted 9.53% of T2DM cases, adding 0.52 QALYs at net cost savings. For pharmacologic comparators, Source 11 and Source 13 (ICER Diabetes Prevention Programs report) reference metformin and GLP-1 receptor agonists in prevention algorithms, and Source 14 notes potential cardiovascular benefits of GLP-1 agonists could improve their cost profile, but no QALY-denominated ICERs for these drugs in prevention are extractable from the snippets. Missing information: (1) direct ICER of intermittent fasting versus metformin, (2) ICER versus GLP-1 agonists, (3) 10-year horizon modeling, and (4) numeric FMD ICER values from van den Burg 2025.", "key_findings": ["No source provides a direct three-way ICER comparing intermittent fasting, metformin, and GLP-1 agonists for T2D prevention (Sources 1-15).", "Fasting-mimicking diet reduced diabetes complications by 11.4% and medication use by 67.2% versus usual care (Source 2, Habka 2025).", "Median ICER for diet and physical activity programs is $13,761/QALY from a health care perspective (Source 9, Duijzer 2019).", "Lifestyle intervention averted 9.53% of T2DM cases, yielding 0.52 additional QALYs at net cost savings (Source 8, Ma 2020).", "GLP-1 agonists' cardiovascular benefits could improve their cost-effectiveness profile, but no prevention ICER is reported (Source 14, ICER 2022)."], "sources_used": ["https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1098301524028468", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12599020/", "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41214519/", "https://d-nb.info/1206089458/34", "https://www.ajmc.com/view/cost-effectiveness-of-varying-weight-loss-interventions-elena-losina-phd", "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7174913/", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/CTAF_DPP_Final_Evidence_Report_072516.pdf", "https://icer.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/ICER_Obesity_Final_Evidence_Report_and_Meeting_Summary_102022.pdf"]}
